---
title: "Shawn Graham"
image: "smg-med-upright.jpeg"
links:
  - label: Carleton U
    url: "https://carleton.ca/history/people/shawn-graham/"
  - label: Twitter
    url: "https://twitter.com/electricarchaeo"
  - label: GitHub
    url: "https://github.com/shawngraham"
  - label: Email
    url: "mailto:shawn.graham@carleton.ca"
  - label: Notebook
    url: "https://electricarchaeology.ca"
output:
  postcards::trestles
---
 
## Bio

Dr. Shawn Graham is a digital archaeologist and Full Professor in the [Department of History at Carleton University in Ottawa, Canada](https://carleton.ca/history). He is also the Programme Coordinator for the [MA specialization in Digital Humanities](https://carleton.ca/dighum). He edits the open access journal [Epoiesen: A Journal for Creative Engagement in History and Archaeology](https://epoiesen.library.carleton.ca/). 

His current major research project, together with his collaborator Damien Huffer, uses neural networks and computer vision to explore the [online trade in human remains](https://bonetrade.github.io). As part of the Computational Research in the Ancient Near East (CRANE) Project from the University of Toronto, he is exploring generative adversarial networks and archaeological photography. His most recent [book](https://www.berghahnbooks.com/title/GrahamEnchantment), 'An Enchantment of Digital Archaeology: Raising the Dead with Agent-Based Models, Archaeogaming and Artificial Intelligence', was published by Berghahn Books. For more on his research, see his [faculty page](https://carleton.ca/history/people/shawn-graham/) or his [publications](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=IU6usq8AAAAJ&hl=en).

His work has been featured in the [Ottawa Citizen](https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/carleton-prof-harnesses-machine-learning-to-explore-the-bone-trade-netherworld/), [WIRED](https://www.wired.co.uk/article/instagram-skull-trade), and [The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2020/11/24/science/artificial-intelligence-archaeology-cnn.html#click=https://t.co/sxyEN0tJtu).

In 2019, he won the [Archaeological Institute of America's Award for Outstanding Work in Digital Archaeology](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/digital-archaeology-award/) for leading the creation of the 'Open Digital Archaeology Textbook Environment', [O-DATE](https://o-date.github.io).

## Education

**University of Reading** | Ph.D. Archaeology | 2002

**University of Reading** | M.A. City of Rome | 1998

**Wilfrid Laurier University** | B.A. Hons Archaeology | 1997

## Current Courses

**HIST3814o** | [Crafting Digital History](https://craftingdh.netlify.app)  

**HIST3000a** | [Introduction to Digital Archaeology](https://digiarch.netlify.app)

**HIST4916a** | [DH + Museums](https://dhmuse.netlify.app)

**HIST5706a** | [Digital History for Public Historians](#)
